explanation: This command uses short-form path notation (double backslashes), omits quotes around the registry key, and substitutes the '/f' flag for confirmation bypass. It still matches the Sigma rule's detection logic.
This command uses quotes around the registry key, substitutes '00' for '0' as an equivalent numeric value, and preserves all required elements for detection.
This command uses the alternate Windows NT path notation (Windows\NT instead of Windows NT), omits quotes, and preserves the essential command structure for detection.
This command uses uppercase command syntax (ADD instead of add), omits quotes, and preserves the required command structure for detection.
